pages.raw_text:
The reason given by officers Of
the Retail Liquor Dealers associa-
ton for opposing the referendum
ordinance now before the city
council is that a special election
might be demanded by those op-
posed to the granting of a saloon
license in some particular place.
The referendum advocates point
out that opponents of the law fail
to realize that 10 per cent Of the
voters would have to sign a petit
ton to the city council before they
could demand the attention of that
body, and that whenever a petit
ton with that percentage of voters
petitions the council at the pres
ent time they can, under ordinary
circumstances, command consider
alton.


AS a matter of fact there is moth
| ing in the ordinance that is I'M
just or unfair to anyone, it is
urged, and the opposition is be-
lieved to come solely from those
interests that seek to secure and
control public patronage and spe-
| cial favors. The officers of the
| referendum league are also con-
vinced that the big corpora tions
which have grown fat and power
full on public patronage have lined
up with the other interests that
are opposing the ordinance. To
secure the support of some of the
local liquor dealers who could not
discover anything in the new Or
dinance directed against the sa.
loon interests Jack Wilmot and
other members of the association
have circulated the report, it is
claimed, that the referendum
movement has been stared by the
advocates Of Sunday closing. The
fact is that there is not today a
local clergyman a member of the
Spokane County Referendum lea.
que. having been originally organ
lied by members of the local
unions upon lines laid down by the
American Federation of Labor and
the local movement is a part of
the big campaign now being in.
augurated by labor throughout the
country. Members of the league
are somewhat gratified in the con.
viction that they have compelled
a roll call and lining up Of all
those interests that have hereto
fore controlled elections and who
now realize that if the reforms ad.
vocated by the local league sue
coed their old game of political
graft Is gone.
